commands.review_summary:
The PR appends nested fetch causes to Chrome CDP diagnostic messages, adds a WSL2 `netsh portproxy` empty-reply hint, covers the formatter path with a focused test, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. for the formatter-level behavior: current main lacks the targeted empty-reply hint, and the linked issue gives concrete WSL2 portproxy symptoms. I did not establish a live Windows/WSL2 portproxy reproduction in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(browser): hint WSL portproxy CDP empty replies
target.body:
Summary
- include nested fetch causes in redacted Chrome CDP diagnostic messages
- add a WSL2 portproxy self-loop hint for empty/closed HTTP CDP replies
- keep the hint off ordinary refused/unreachable failures

Fixes #59209.

Verification
- `/Users/steipete/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v24.13.0/installation/bin/node ./node_modules/.bin/tsx -e "...formatChromeCdpDiagnostic assertion..."`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Behavior addressed: WSL2 users whose Windows CDP port is owned by stale `netsh portproxy`/`svchost` now get a targeted empty-reply diagnostic instead of a generic unreachable message.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node 24.13.0 with the browser diagnostic formatter executed directly; GitHub CI will run the full repository gates.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: direct Node 24 `tsx` assertion for `safeChromeCdpErrorMessage` and `formatChromeCdpDiagnostic`, `git diff --check`, and Codex autoreview local mode.
Evidence after fix: the direct assertion verified the WSL2 hint appears for `fetch failed: other side closed` and does not appear for `fetch failed: connect ECONNREFUSED`.
Observed result after fix: direct assertion printed `cdp hint ok`; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
What was not tested: a live WSL2 Windows Chrome portproxy setup; the change is diagnostic text only and does not alter CDP connection behavior.
target.author: steipete
